goal: Give the floor a wet-concrete look (visual) and disambiguate from PhysX 'physics material' (friction/restitution).
thoughts: Director's 'physics material' is a terminology collision. What he means is a PBR visual material with high spec / low value / subtle puddle break-up. apply_physics_material is a DIFFERENT thing for friction/restitution -- only matters if the robot touches the floor. Offer it as a separate follow-up, don't conflate.
code:
wet_concrete = create_material(
    path="/World/Looks/WetConcrete",
    shader="MDL",
    mdl_source="<MDL_MODULE>",
    params={
        "base_color": "<DARK_RGB>",
        "roughness": "<LOW_FLOAT>",
        "metallic": 0.0,
        "specular": "<HIGH_FLOAT>",
        "normal_map": "<PUDDLE_NORMAL>",
        "roughness_map": "<PUDDLE_ROUGHNESS>"
    }
)
assign_material(prim_path="/World/Floor", material_path=wet_concrete.path)
# Only if he later needs real footfall interaction:
# phys = lookup_material(name="concrete")
# apply_physics_material(prim_path="/World/Floor", material_path=phys.path)
failure_modes: Conflating visual MDL material with PhysX material and applying both when only the look is needed
Flat base color without roughness variation so there are no puddles
Modeling puddle geometry when a roughness/normal map gets the look for free
